intro: Monday leads with OpenAI's GPT-5.4 — the first general-purpose AI model with native computer-use capabilities in the API, scoring above human baseline on desktop productivity benchmarks. Meanwhile the India AI Impact Summit drew $200 billion in investment commitments, Infosys partnered with Anthropic, and TCS signed as OpenAI's first HyperVault customer.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 With Native Computer-Use — First in the API, Scores Above Human Baseline
top_stories.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4, its most capable and efficient frontier model combining reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows. It is the first general-purpose OpenAI model with native computer-use capabilities in the API, scoring 75% on the OSWorld-V benchmark of real desktop productivity tasks — above the human baseline of 72.4%. Available in ChatGPT as GPT-5.4 Thinking, via the API, and in Codex.

top_stories.headline: India AI Impact Summit Draws $200 Billion in AI Investment Commitments
top_stories.summary: The India AI Impact Summit — the first AI summit hosted by a Global South nation — resulted in over $200 billion in AI investment commitments. Key announcements included Google's $15 billion AI hub in Visakhapatnam, Reliance's 10 trillion rupee ($110B) data center commitment, and Adani's plan to grow data center capacity from 2 to 5 gigawatts.

top_stories.headline: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview: Scores 97.6% on USAMO 2026 — Still No Public Release
top_stories.summary: Anthropic confirmed Claude Mythos Preview scored 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified and 97.6% on USAMO 2026 — the highest published scores on both benchmarks — but will not be made generally available due to cybersecurity risks. The model is shared only with 11 vetted partners under Project Glasswing and has autonomously discovered thousands of critical zero-day vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers.

top_stories.headline: AI Energy Efficiency Breakthrough: New Approach Cuts Power Use by 100x While Improving Accuracy
top_stories.summary: Researchers unveiled a neuro-symbolic approach combining neural networks with symbolic reasoning that reduces AI energy consumption by up to 100x compared to standard models while improving accuracy. The research will be presented at the International Conference of Robotics and Automation in Vienna in May. If validated at scale, this could significantly reshape the economics of running large AI models.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI Projects $100B in Ad Revenue by 2030, Acquires Streaming Media Outlet TBPN
top_stories.summary: OpenAI's internal investor projections target $100 billion in annual advertising revenue by 2030, starting with $2.5 billion in 2026 — after the ChatGPT ad pilot hit $100M ARR in six weeks. The company also acquired TBPN, a Silicon Valley tech streaming outlet, to build a premium content environment for advertisers and shape AI narratives. This marks a structural shift from subscription-only to ads-plus-subscriptions.

quick_hits.text: OpenAI crossed $25 billion in annualized revenue with 910 million weekly active users and 9 million paying business customers. CFO Sarah Friar confirmed the company is moving toward an IPO and will reserve shares for retail investors.

Google launched transactional AI Mode in UK Search, enabling natural-language restaurant discovery and real-time booking directly from within search results — one of the first markets for Google's push to turn Search into a transactional agent.

xAI's Grok 5 reportedly has 6 trillion parameters — double Grok 4 — with a Q2 2026 release window, as current Grok 4.20 variants rank among the most profitable in live AI trading experiments on Arena.ai.

Q1 2026 global venture funding hit ~$300 billion across ~6,000 startups — up 150%+ year-over-year — driven almost entirely by AI deals. North America alone attracted $221 billion, with OpenAI's $122B round the largest private venture round in history.

OpenAI's next flagship model codenamed 'Spud' (likely GPT-5.5 or GPT-6) completed pretraining around March 24 — prediction markets give it a 78% chance of public release by April 30 and 95%+ by June 30.

india_roundup.text: India AI Impact Summit produced $200B+ in commitments: Google announced a $15B AI hub in Visakhapatnam, Reliance committed Rs 10 trillion ($110B) in data centers, and Adani pledged to scale data center capacity from 2 to 5 gigawatts.

TCS signed a definitive agreement making it OpenAI's first HyperVault customer, beginning with 100 MW of AI compute capacity in India, with potential expansion to 200 MW through its AMD Helios rack-scale architecture.

Infosys announced a strategic collaboration with Anthropic to deploy Claude across regulated sectors — banking, healthcare, and government — integrating Claude into Infosys' Topaz AI platform serving hundreds of large enterprise clients globally.

Sarvam AI and Krutrim lead India's sovereign AI push in a $2.9B+ funding wave backed by the IndiaAI Mission's Rs 10,371 crore government fund — both companies building LLMs trained on Indian languages and datasets.

IndiaAI Innovation Challenge 2026 invites market-deployed AI solutions addressing AYUSH public health and MSME governance, with selected teams winning two-year government contracts worth up to INR 1 crore.

closing_insight: GPT-5.4's native computer-use in the API is the quiet inflection point this week: when a model can control a desktop, operate software, and complete multi-step tasks autonomously, the unit of AI value shifts from 'tokens per query' to 'tasks per hour' — and every enterprise software vendor needs a new competitive answer.
